comment:
QA pre-review — RECOMMEND.

Frontmatter verified: title='ToolContext Migration Completion + Documentation Reconciliation' (present, non-empty); revision_summary describes the bundled scope and the SourceTool crash trigger.

Required sections all present and substantive: §1 Purpose, §2 Scope (2.1/2.2/2.3), §3 Current State (with §3.1 audit table), §4 Proposed State, §5 Change Description (5.1-5.8), §6 Justification, §7 Impact Assessment with §7.4 Development Controls and §7.5 Qualified State Continuity (correctly populated for the FLOW-STATE-1.0 -> 1.1 transition), §8 Testing Summary, §9 Implementation Plan (8 phases), §10 Execution (13 EIs), §11/§12.

No author-time {{...}} placeholders remain. Execution-time [SUMMARY]/[Pass/Fail]/[PERFORMER]/[DATE] markers are appropriate for DRAFT.

Spot-checked factual claims against current code:
  - flow-state/ui/source_tool.py:201 crash site (self.app.scene.sketch.entities) — VERIFIED.
  - flow-state/ui/tools.py:87 (self.app = ctx._app) — VERIFIED.
  - flow-state/ui/input_handler.py:4-9 stale 5-layer protocol docstring — VERIFIED.
  - flow-state/engine/compiler.py:7 'Data flow is ONE-WAY' wording — VERIFIED.
  - flow-state/ui/tools.py:928 and :978 interaction_data mutation sites — VERIFIED.
  - SDLC-FLOW-RS v1.0 and SDLC-FLOW-RTM v1.0 EFFECTIVE — VERIFIED via qms status.

Code-CR-specific gates correctly enforced:
  - RS must be EFFECTIVE before Phase 3 (line 342, EI-3 precedes EI-4).
  - RTM must be EFFECTIVE before Phase 7 (lines 379-382, EI-10 precedes EI-11).
  - Regular merge commit (--no-ff), not squash, per SOP-005 §7.1.3 (line 384).
  - Annotated tag FLOW-STATE-1.1 at the merge commit (EI-11).
  - Submodule pointer advance only after merge (EI-11, §7.4 item 7).

EI ordering integrity:
  - EI-4 (add ctx.add_process_object) precedes EI-5 (SourceTool migration that depends on it) — correct dependency.
  - EI-5/EI-6 (per-tool migration) precede EI-7 (delete self.app passthrough) — correct; reversing this order would crash unmigrated tools, and §5.2 explicitly calls out this hazard.
  - EI-9 (integration verification) precedes EI-10 (RTM update with qualified commit hash) — correct; the qualified commit cannot be cited until verification is recorded.

VR flags appropriately placed on EI-5, EI-6, EI-7, EI-9 (the behavior-affecting EIs). EI-4 (pure ctx API addition with no caller) and EI-8 (docstring-only) correctly omit VR.

Scope integrity: §2.2 names exactly 10 changes, all of which trace to the unifying theme (Air Gap closure). §5.4 explicitly states 'No other API expansion is permitted by this CR' — scope is closed, not open-ended. §5.6.4 bounds the CLAUDE.md audit pass to drift discovered while editing §§2.3/6.2/7 with §§5.4/5.5 named as already-known candidates. I do not see scope creep.

Honest gap acknowledgment: §7.4 item 5 acknowledges no CI exists on flow-state and identifies the post-integration-verification commit as the qualified commit until CI is added (queued CR). §8 Automated Verification is honestly marked 'Not applicable.' This is the right call rather than fabricating a CI gate.

Bundling justification (§6) is sound: all five concerns are different projections of finishing the Air Gap; splitting into five CRs would multiply review overhead without improving comprehension. The crash on first post-CR-111 use is a legitimate forcing function.

Reviewers assigned: tu_ui (owns tools.py, source_tool.py, input_handler.py docstring), tu_scene (owns tool_context.py and scene.py orchestration narrative), tu_sim (owns compiler.py docstring and physics-step phasing), tu_sketch (owns sketch.interaction_data ownership and §5.6.1 attribution fix). tu_input does not exist as a valid QMS reviewer; input_handler concerns folded into tu_ui's scope (it lives in flow-state/ui/).

Minor observation (not a defect): §5.6.4 says 'the entire CLAUDE.md technical guide (§§1-9) is read against current code state' alongside the bounding clause 'bounded by anything else discovered while doing §§2.3, 6.2, 7.' These are reconcilable (read everything, fix only what surfaces while doing the targeted edits), but the executor should treat the bounding clause as authoritative to avoid open-ended audit creep. No revision required.

Recommendation: pre-review RECOMMEND. Pending TU concurrence.

Technical review (CAD/Geometry domain) per SOP-002.

Verified against flow-state/ code at FLOW-STATE-1.0:
- sketch.py:33 confirms interaction_data is a Sketch field (not Session), so the CLAUDE.md §2.3 -> §5.1 relocation is accurate.
- tools.py:87 (self.app = ctx._app), and the four mutation sites at 788/950/978/1061 plus the read at 928 are correctly identified.
- source_tool.py uses __init__(self, app) and is unmigrated; the AttributeError at the cited reach is real.
- solver.py:223-248 already consumes point_idx/handle_t/target, so the proposed set_interaction_data(*, handle_t=None, point_idx=None) signature extension is shape-compatible with the solver and requires no solver changes.
- scene.py:265-345 confirms 8 numbered orchestration phases (the docstring itself documents 1-8), validating §5.6.3.
- input_handler.py:108-129 confirms the actual 4-layer order is System -> Modals -> Global -> HUD, validating §5.6.2.
- compiler.py:7 'Data flow is ONE-WAY' is contradicted by lines 19-23 of the same docstring (tether two-way coupling); §5.5 wording fix is correct.
- REQ-FS-ARCH-004 and REQ-FS-CAD-003 wording in SDLC-FLOW-RS v1.0 fits the proposed revisions; the current RTM v1.0 evidence row for REQ-FS-CAD-003 already acknowledges that the facade exists but is unused, making this a clean closure rather than a scope shift.

Domain assessment: this CR closes a real Air Gap hole on the Sketch side (interaction_data is Sketch-owned model state; direct tool writes bypass the facade). The PBD solver and constraint pipeline are not modified; the change is exclusively at the tool->Sketch interface. EI ordering (migrate then delete passthrough) is correct and prevents a mid-execution build break.

One nuance worth flagging to the executor (not blocking): replacing the direct Coincident(...) instantiation in source_tool.py with ctx.create_coincident_command() routes through sketch.try_create_constraint('COINCIDENT', ...), which performs validation the direct path does not. This is an improvement, but the EI-5 VR should sanity-check that the Ctrl-snap path still creates the expected coincident constraint end-to-end.

Recommend approval.

TU-SCENE review (orchestration / Scene / commands / ToolContext domain). Verified key factual claims against flow-state code: (1) interaction_data lives on Sketch (model/sketch.py:33), not Session — CR's CLAUDE.md §2.3 correction accurate. (2) Scene.update at core/scene.py:265-345 is 8 phases as listed in §5.6.3; the docstring at lines 270-279 already enumerates them. (3) SourceTool crash mechanism is real — flow_state_app.py:217 passes ctx to every tool constructor including SourceTool, but SourceTool uses self.app = app, so every self.app.scene.X reach hits a ToolContext (AttributeError as reported). (4) The four interaction_data mutation sites (tools.py 788, 950, 978, 1061) and read site (928) match grep exactly. (5) §5.3.1 set_interaction_data signature extension is genuinely necessary, not just stylistic — current facade omits point_idx but the solver (solver.py:223-248) branches on point_idx vs handle_t for EDIT-mode vs body-drag; tools.py:978 sets point_idx today, so the existing facade cannot replace direct mutation without the signature change. (6) Scene.add_process_object exists at scene.py:122; the proposed ctx wrapper is sound. Domain-specific assessment: Command pattern integrity preserved (no new mutation bypasses Commands; interaction_data is correctly classified as transient solver-input, not historized state). Orchestration correctness preserved (no Scene.update ordering changes; CR explicitly notes Scene continues to read sketch.interaction_data directly because Scene is not a tool — correct asymmetry). State ownership clarified (moves interaction_data callout to its actual home). Air Gap strengthened (removing self.app = ctx._app and routing interaction_data through facade closes the last tool-level bypass). Sequencing sound (EI-4 → EI-5 → EI-6 → EI-7 correctly avoids removing the passthrough before tools are migrated; CR explicitly addresses this at §5.2). Minor concerns: §3.1 audit-table count (~150 self.app reaches in tools.py) overstates actual count of 99 — doesn't change feasibility but is imprecise. §5.6.4 'targeted audit pass' is mildly open-ended; bounded acceptably. CR could note explicitly that Scene.update reading sketch.interaction_data directly is a deliberate, non-policed exception. None of these concerns are blocking. No architectural concerns in the orchestration domain. Recommend approval.

TU-UI review: I support this CR's purpose and architectural direction (closing the Air Gap, fixing the SourceTool crash, adopting the interaction_data facade). The cited line numbers, the SourceTool crash diagnosis, the input_handler.py 5-vs-4-layer drift, the compiler.py 'ONE-WAY' wording drift, and the scene.py 8-phase orchestration loop all check out against the code. The audit table in §3.1 is materially accurate at the category level. Plan ordering (add ctx methods first, migrate SourceTool, migrate tools.py, then delete passthrough) is correct.

REQUESTED UPDATES (UI-domain concerns the executor will hit):

1. HIDDEN COUPLING NOT IN §3.1: tools.py defines @property accessors that read through self.app — LineTool.sketch / LineTool.scene at tools.py:277-283, and SelectTool.sketch / SelectTool.scene at tools.py:741-747. After EI-7 deletes 'self.app = ctx._app' at line 87, every call site that uses self.sketch / self.scene (and there are many: 300, 309, 364, 365, 376, 383, 386, 417, 423, 426, 433, 436, 484, 485, 499, 502, 515, 518, 572, 573, 588, 591, 624, 627, 633, 685, 686, 692, 785, 788, 928, 929, 950, 968, 978, 985, 988, 994, 1010, 1015, 1022, 1061, 1071, etc.) will break with AttributeError. The CR §5.2 says 'mechanical substitution' but this hidden subclass-level coupling needs explicit treatment. Two options: (a) redirect the @property accessors to use self.ctx._get_sketch() / self.ctx._get_scene() (works but perpetuates the cached-shortcut pattern), or (b) remove the @property accessors and migrate all self.sketch/self.scene call sites to ctx.execute(...) / ctx._get_sketch() inline. Either is acceptable; the CR should pick one and add it to §5.2 / EI-6 explicitly. Without this, EI-7 will fail.

2. CTX.CLEAR_CONSTRAINT_UI() IS NOT A DROP-IN REPLACEMENT: tools.py:1378-1384 (_clear_constraint_ui method) calls builder.reset() which clears _pending_type, _target_walls, _target_points, _snap_target. But ctx.clear_constraint_ui() at tool_context.py:474-486 only clears pending_type and snap_target — it does NOT clear _target_walls / _target_points. There is also an attribute-name mismatch: tools.py sets btn.active = False; ctx.clear_constraint_ui sets btn.is_active = False. §3.1 lists this as 'already exists' but the semantics differ. Recommended: either (a) extend ctx.clear_constraint_ui() to call builder.reset() and use the correct attribute name, or (b) keep the existing tool method but route its single self.app.input_handler reach through ctx (e.g., expose a ctx.iter_constraint_buttons() helper, or call ctx.constraint_builder.reset() and have the tool reach button list via a new ctx accessor). Pick one and document it in §5.4 alongside the new add_process_object / has_interaction_data methods.

3. SET_INTERACTION_DATA SIGNATURE IN §5.3: §5.3.1 proposes 'def set_interaction_data(self, target_pos, entity_idx, *, handle_t=None, point_idx=None)'. Good signature. But §5.3 line 131 shows the call as 'self.ctx.set_interaction_data(target_pos, entity_idx, point_idx, handle_t=None)' — with point_idx positional. After the *, point_idx is keyword-only. This call would TypeError. Either drop the * (allow positional), or rewrite the call site as 'self.ctx.set_interaction_data(target_pos, entity_idx, point_idx=pt_idx, handle_t=None)'. Minor wording fix.

4. SOURCETOOL'S _create_source RAISES A SECOND DESIGN QUESTION: §5.1 substitutes the inline Coincident construction at source_tool.py:176-179 with ctx.create_coincident_command(). However, source_tool.py:170 calls source.get_handle_indices(self.app.scene.sketch) which the CR plans to migrate to self.ctx._get_sketch() — that is fine. But note that SourceTool also calls AddConstraintCommand directly via 'from core.commands import AddConstraintCommand' (line 155). After this CR the import would be unused if create_coincident_command returns the AddConstraintCommand for us. Confirm the import is removed in EI-5; this is the kind of scar tissue the CR should keep an eye on.

5. SCOPE NOTE: I'd appreciate explicit confirmation in §5.2 (or a new §5.2.1) that EI-6 also surveys for any remaining tool-side cached references to app sub-objects beyond .sketch/.scene/.sim — e.g., are there cached self.session, self.layout, self.constraint_builder in tools.py? A quick grep on the test branch before EI-7 would catch any I missed in this review.

NON-BLOCKING OBSERVATIONS:

- The 8-phase orchestration loop is already documented in scene.py:265-289 itself, so the CLAUDE.md §7 update is genuinely just bringing the architecture doc into line with what the orchestrator already announces in its own docstring. Good fix.
- §6.2 reordering (System → Modals → Global → HUD) is correct and the rationale comment at input_handler.py:117-119 already captures the 'why'.
- §5.3 facade adoption (set/update/clear interaction_data) is the right shape and the existing solver.py:223-248 already handles the dict format produced by either signature.
- I'm comfortable with the bundling (Air Gap completion + CLAUDE.md drift) — these are thematically the same work.

Overall: solid CR; please address the 5 items above (especially #1, which is a real ordering hazard for EI-7) and re-route. Once these are addressed I anticipate recommending.

Verified the Simulation/Compiler/Engine claims against actual flow-state code. Findings:

(1) Compiler docstring revision (engine/compiler.py line 7, §5.5): The current 'Data flow is ONE-WAY: Sketch -> Simulation (never reverse)' wording is internally inconsistent with lines 19-22 of the same file, which already describe 'Tether System (Two-Way Coupling)'. The proposed nuanced framing - Compiler itself is one-way (reads sketch, writes atom arrays), system as a whole becomes two-way once tethered atoms participate in physics - is technically accurate. The reverse data flow lives in physics_core.py:413-428 (tether kernel applies Newton's 3rd-law reaction force + torque to parent entity) and scene.py:_run_physics_coupling (entity.integrate writes back to sketch entities). Correct fix.

(2) §7 Orchestration Loop (8 phases): Verified against scene.py:265-345. Phase enumeration in CR §5.6.3 matches exactly: topology check, driver update, sync_entity_arrays + sync_static_atoms, _run_physics_coupling, constraint solve, post-constraint sync, process_objects, simulation.step. The 5-phase narrative in CLAUDE.md §7 is genuinely stale.

(3) §5.6.4 targeted audit re: CLAUDE.md §5.4 Compiler: Appropriate scope. Current §5.4 describes only 'static particles'; the Compiler genuinely produces both is_static=1 (static, follows geometry via sync_static_atoms_to_geometry teleport) and is_static=3 (tethered, two-way force coupling via apply_tether_forces). The static-vs-tethered split is materially distinct - static atoms have unidirectional teleport-from-geometry; tethered atoms have spring + reaction-force two-way coupling. Worth documenting.

(4) SourceTool crash claim (§2.1): Verified. source_tool.py:32-33 stores constructor arg as self.app; flow_state_app.py:217 passes ctx (a ToolContext, not the app); ToolContext has no .scene attribute, so line 201 (self.app.scene.sketch.entities) is guaranteed to fail on first invocation of _get_snapped. The crash is real and the diagnosis is correct.

(5) Simulation-domain integrity: The proposed migration touches no physics kernel logic, particle data structures, integration order, or compile/rebuild pipeline. New ctx.add_process_object is a thin passthrough to existing Scene.add_process_object (scene.py:122). set_interaction_data signature extension (adding point_idx kwarg) is backward-compatible: no current callers exist; solver at solver.py:225 reads via .get('point_idx') so absence returns None. No simulation-side concerns.

Minor observation (not a blocker): The §3.1 audit table maps ~19 'self.app.sim.X' reaches to ctx.world_size and ctx.snapshot_particles, but BrushTool at tools.py:153 instantiates ParticleBrush(self.app.sim), passing the entire Simulation object as a constructor argument. This is not a simple property reach. Migration path: use the existing ctx.paint_particles() / ctx.erase_particles() methods directly (cleaner) rather than caching a local ParticleBrush. The CR's 'mechanical substitution' framing slightly understates this case, but it remains tractable within stated scope and will surface during EI-6. Heads-up for the executor.

Recommend approval. Domain claims are accurate, the two-way coupling characterization is correct, and the simulation engine is not adversely affected.

QA pre-review of v0.2 — REQUEST UPDATES.

Frontmatter verified: title='ToolContext Migration Completion + Documentation Reconciliation' (present, non-empty); revision_summary describes the v0.2 changes addressing tu_ui's three numbered blocking findings plus the non-blocking pickups from tu_scene/tu_sketch/tu_sim.

Verification of v0.2 changes against tu_ui v0.1 findings:

Finding #1 (hidden coupling — @property sketch / @property scene at tools.py:277-283 and 741-747): ADDRESSED. New §3.1 'Hidden coupling' subsection added (~22 + ~18 secondary callsites enumerated). §5.2 'Subclass property accessor migration' subsection shows the property bodies rewritten to delegate through ctx._get_sketch() / ctx._get_scene(). §7.1 file table updated. EI-6 description updated.

Finding #2 (ctx.clear_constraint_ui semantic mismatch — missing builder.reset(); btn.is_active vs btn.active): ADDRESSED. New §5.4 'Fix to existing clear_constraint_ui()' subsection identifies both defects and shows the corrected implementation. §3.1 audit-table note flags the bug. EI-4 description includes the fix. §7.1 file table for tool_context.py reflects the fix.

Finding #3 (§5.3 / §5.3.1 signature/call inconsistency — point_idx positional vs keyword-only): ADDRESSED. §5.3 lines 169-170 now show both call sites using keyword form (point_idx=, handle_t=) consistent with the keyword-only signature in §5.3.1. The inconsistency is cleanly resolved.

Finding #4 (SourceTool unused import scar tissue): ADDRESSED. §5.1 last paragraph explicitly directs EI-5 to remove 'from core.commands import AddConstraintCommand' and 'from model.constraints import Coincident'. EI-5 description and §7.1 file table both list the cleanup.

NEW DEFECTS / NOT-YET-ADDRESSED (warrant a v0.3):

(A) §2.3 (Files Affected) internal inconsistencies — UNRESOLVED. The frontmatter explicitly claims 'corrected tools.py audit count (99 not ~150)', and §3.1 (line 91) and §7.1 (line 327) both say '99'. But §2.3 line 56 still reads 'Replace ~150 self.app.X reaches'. Same drift on source_tool.py: §2.3 line 55 says '~28 reaches' while §3.1 / §7.1 say '25'. Beyond the count drift, §2.3 line 56 does not mention the @property accessor migration, the BrushTool ParticleBrush replacement, or the _clear_constraint_ui body replacement — all of which were added to §3.1 / §5.2 / §7.1 in v0.2. §2.3 line 57 says 'optionally add has_interaction_data()' (stale 'optionally' wording from v0.1) and is silent on both the clear_constraint_ui() fix and the set_interaction_data signature extension that §5.4 / §5.3.1 add definitively in v0.2. Net effect: §2.3 reads as a v0.1 summary while the rest of the document reads as v0.2. Reviewers (and the future executor) drawing scope from §2.3 will form a different mental model than those drawing scope from §3.1/§5.2/§5.4/§7.1. Fix: rewrite §2.3 to match the v0.2 scope captured downstream.

(B) §5.2 line 157 has a residual error introduced in v0.2 — UNRESOLVED. The added prose reads 'EI-5 migrates SourceTool to depend on the ctx.add_process_object() method **and the ctx.clear_constraint_ui fix that EI-4 adds**.' SourceTool does not call clear_constraint_ui — that method is consumed by tools.py (per §5.2 line 151 and EI-6). The dependency claim is incorrect. Fix: drop the 'and the ctx.clear_constraint_ui fix' clause from line 157.

(C) tu_ui Finding #5 (scope survey for cached references to app sub-objects beyond .sketch/.scene/.sim — e.g., self.session, self.layout, self.constraint_builder) — NOT ADDRESSED. tu_ui labelled this a 'SCOPE NOTE' in the requested-updates list and asked for 'explicit confirmation in §5.2 (or a new §5.2.1) that EI-6 also surveys for any remaining tool-side cached references.' v0.2's frontmatter and body do not mention this. This is the lightest-weight of tu_ui's five findings, but tu_ui's closing line was 'please address the 5 items above'. v0.2 explicitly addresses items 1-3 in the frontmatter and item 4 implicitly via §5.1, but is silent on item 5. Fix: either add a brief §5.2.1 (or a sentence inside §5.2) acknowledging that EI-6 will grep for and resolve any cached self.session / self.layout / self.constraint_builder / etc. before EI-7 deletes the passthrough, or document the alternative that no such caches exist (verified via grep at the FLOW-STATE-1.0 baseline).

NON-DEFECT OBSERVATIONS:

- v0.2's non-blocking pickups from the three TUs that recommended cleanly are visibly integrated: §3.1 audit count corrected (99/25), §3.1 + §5.2 explicitly call out the BrushTool ParticleBrush(self.app.sim) constructor-argument special case and route it through ctx.paint_particles / ctx.erase_particles, EI-5 VR scope extends to Ctrl-snap Coincident verification, and §5.3 explicitly notes Scene's direct read of sketch.interaction_data as a deliberate non-policed exception.
- Code-CR-specific gates remain correctly enforced: RS EFFECTIVE before Phase 3 (EI-3 → EI-4); RTM EFFECTIVE before Phase 7 (EI-10 → EI-11); regular merge commit (--no-ff), not squash; annotated tag FLOW-STATE-1.1 at the merge commit; submodule pointer advance only after merge.
- EI ordering integrity preserved: EI-4 (add ctx.add_process_object + clear_constraint_ui fix) precedes EI-5 (SourceTool migration that depends on add_process_object); EI-5/EI-6 (per-tool migration including @property accessor rewrite) precede EI-7 (delete self.app passthrough). The hazard called out in the prior cycle (deleting passthrough before completing migration) remains correctly mitigated.
- VR flags appropriately placed on EI-5, EI-6, EI-7, EI-9. EI-4 (pure ctx API addition with no caller) and EI-8 (docstring-only) correctly omit VR.
- Scope integrity: §5.4 explicitly states 'No other API expansion is permitted by this CR' — scope is closed, not open-ended. §5.6.4 bounds the CLAUDE.md audit pass to drift discovered while editing §§2.3/6.2/7.

Reviewers re-assigned: tu_ui (owns tools.py / source_tool.py / input_handler.py — also the original requester of v0.2 changes, must concur the changes adequately address findings 1-3), tu_scene (owns tool_context.py and scene.py orchestration narrative — should re-verify §5.4 fix and the §5.2 EI-5 dependency wording), tu_sim (owns compiler.py docstring and physics-step phasing — minimal v0.2 delta in this domain, brief re-confirmation expected), tu_sketch (owns sketch.interaction_data ownership and §5.6.1 attribution — minimal v0.2 delta in this domain).

Recommendation: please address (A), (B), (C) and re-route. Once §2.3 is synchronized with the rest of v0.2, the §5.2 EI-5 dependency wording is corrected, and finding #5 is addressed (or confirmed not applicable), I anticipate recommending.

QA pre-review of v0.3 — RECOMMEND.

Frontmatter verified: title='ToolContext Migration Completion + Documentation Reconciliation' (present, non-empty); revision_summary describes the v0.3 changes addressing the three v0.2-cycle findings (A) §2.3 sync, (B) §5.2 EI-5 dependency wording, (C) tu_ui finding #5 cached-sub-object audit, and explicitly notes that v0.2 changes (subclass property migration, ctx.clear_constraint_ui fix, signature/call consistency) are all preserved.

Verification of v0.3 changes against v0.2-cycle QA findings:

Finding (A) — §2.3 (Files Affected) sync with §7.1: ADDRESSED.
  - source_tool.py count: §2.3 line 58 now reads '25 self.app.X reaches' (was '~28' in v0.2). Matches §3.1 and §7.1.
  - tools.py count: §2.3 line 59 now reads 'Replace 99 self.app.X reaches' (was '~150' in v0.2). Matches §3.1 and §7.1.
  - §2.3 line 59 now lists the @property accessor body rewrites (lines 277-283 and 741-747), BrushTool's ParticleBrush replacement with ctx.paint_particles()/ctx.erase_particles(), the four interaction_data mutation site migrations, the _clear_constraint_ui body replacement, and the deletion of self.app = ctx._app at line 87.
  - §2.3 line 60 (tool_context.py) now reads 'Add add_process_object(obj) method; add has_interaction_data() query; extend set_interaction_data signature with keyword-only point_idx; fix existing clear_constraint_ui() to call builder.reset() and use btn.active' — the stale 'optionally add has_interaction_data()' wording from v0.2 is gone, and the clear_constraint_ui fix and signature extension are now both explicit.
  Net: §2.3 reads as a v0.3 summary consistent with §3.1 / §5.2 / §5.4 / §7.1.

Finding (B) — §5.2 line 162 (EI-5 dependency wording): ADDRESSED.
  Previous v0.2 text claimed 'EI-5 migrates SourceTool to depend on the ctx.add_process_object() method **and the ctx.clear_constraint_ui fix that EI-4 adds**' — incorrect because SourceTool does not consume clear_constraint_ui. v0.3 §5.2 line 162 reads: 'EI-5 migrates SourceTool to depend on the ctx.add_process_object() method that EI-4 adds. (SourceTool does not consume clear_constraint_ui; that method is consumed only by tools.py in EI-6.)' — the wrong dependency claim is removed and a clarifying parenthetical now states the correct ownership boundary.

Finding (C) — tu_ui finding #5 (cached app-sub-object audit): ADDRESSED.
  v0.3 §5.2 contains a new 'Pre-deletion audit for cached app sub-objects' paragraph (line 158) documenting that a grep over the FLOW-STATE-1.0 baseline for 'self.<X> = ' in tools.py and source_tool.py (covering session, layout, constraint_builder, sound_manager, input_handler, sim, scene, sketch, app) returns exactly two matches: tools.py:87 (self.app = ctx._app) and source_tool.py:33 (self.app = app), both retired by this CR. The audit explicitly states that no cached self.session / self.layout / self.constraint_builder sub-objects exist that would silently break after EI-7. This directly satisfies tu_ui's request for either explicit confirmation that EI-6 surveys for caches or documentation that no such caches exist.

Independent review of v0.3 for new defects:

- No author-time {{...}} placeholders. No TBD/TODO/FIXME/XXX markers in the body. Execution-time [SUMMARY]/[Pass/Fail]/[PERFORMER]/[DATE] markers in §10 are appropriate for DRAFT.
- Cross-section consistency spot-checks all clean:
  - §2.2 item 5, §2.3 line 60, §5.4, §7.1 row for tool_context.py, and EI-4 description all describe the same four ToolContext API changes (add_process_object, has_interaction_data, set_interaction_data signature extension with keyword-only point_idx, clear_constraint_ui fix to call builder.reset() and use btn.active).
  - §2.2 item 2, §2.3 line 59, §3.1 audit table, §5.2, §7.1 row for tools.py, and EI-6 description all describe the same tools.py migration scope (99 reaches, property-accessor body rewrites, BrushTool ParticleBrush replacement, _clear_constraint_ui body replacement, four interaction_data mutation sites).

TU-SCENE third-cycle review (v0.3). RECOMMEND. Domain scope verified: orchestration (Scene), commands, tool-context facade, and process-objects routing.

Substantive findings:
1. Scene.update 8-phase loop documented in §5.6.3 matches scene.py:265-345 exactly. The new CLAUDE.md §7 wording correctly identifies phases 3 (sync entity arrays + static atoms), 4 (physics coupling sub-loop), 6 (post-constraint re-sync), and 7 (process objects) as the additions over the legacy 5-step description.

2. §5.3 'Deliberate non-policed exception' — Scene.update reading sketch.interaction_data directly at scene.py:324 — is architecturally correct. Scene owns the model; the Air Gap polices UI-layer mutation, not Scene's internal orchestration. The post-CR architecture (tools mutate via facade; Scene/solver read directly) is the right invariant.

3. ctx.add_process_object delegates to the existing Scene.add_process_object at scene.py:122 — confirmed present, no Scene-side changes needed for §5.4.

4. ctx.clear_constraint_ui fix at §5.4 is well-grounded: btn.active is the canonical attribute (six call sites in app/ and ui/), and tool_context.py:486 is the only site using the broken btn.is_active form. The current ctx.clear_constraint_ui is silently no-op for button visual reset, which §5.4 corrects alongside the builder.reset() completeness fix.

5. Verified counts in §3.1 and §2.3 — 99 self.app.X reaches in tools.py and 25 in source_tool.py match grep at FLOW-STATE-1.0 baseline. Internal consistency between §2.3 and §7.1 (the v0.3 fix) is now clean.

6. §5.3.1 set_interaction_data signature extension (keyword-only point_idx) is backward-compatible (no current callers); the underlying dict shape matches what model/solver.py:223-248 already consumes per the CR's claim.

7. EI sequencing (EI-4 adds API, EI-5 migrates SourceTool, EI-6 migrates tools.py incl. property accessors and BrushTool, EI-7 deletes passthrough) preserves the no-tool-ever-broken invariant. EI-7 cannot precede EI-6 — the CR correctly enforces this.

8. The §5.2 pre-deletion audit (no cached self.session, self.layout, etc.) plus the property-accessor migration through ctx._get_sketch / ctx._get_scene closes the silent-breakage risk that retiring self.app=ctx._app would otherwise create.

No domain concerns. The orchestration spine remains sound; commands remain the exclusive mutation path; transient/persistent state classification is preserved; and the Scene-update phase narrative will be brought into agreement with the code. Approve for execution.

The substantive sketch/solver/interaction_data content is unchanged from v0.1 (which I recommended); v0.2/v0.3 changes (subclass property migration, ctx.clear_constraint_ui fix, signature/call consistency, file-count corrections, dropped wrong dependency claim, cached-sub-object audit note) sharpen scope without altering the in-domain technical correctness. TU-SIM v0.3 review (third cycle): RECOMMEND. Re-verified domain content against v0.3 draft. Simulation/compiler/physics scope is unchanged from v0.1/v0.2 in substance — v0.3 deltas (§7.1/§2.3 count reconciliation, §5.2 dependency claim removal, cached-sub-object audit note) are administrative and outside my primary domain. Domain checks repeated against current flow-state code: (1) compiler.py:7 wording correction is technically precise — Compiler module is one-way (CAD → atom arrays); two-way coupling lives at the physics step via apply_tether_forces_pbd on tethered atoms (is_static=3) per simulation.py:23,57,63-69; (2) §7 8-phase orchestration loop verified line-by-line against scene.py:265-345 docstring and implementation, including the correctly-noted 'solver runs if interaction_data is set' branch at scene.py:324; (3) BrushTool refactor to route through ctx.paint_particles/erase_particles is behaviorally inert — Scene already owns ParticleBrush(self.simulation) at scene.py:82, so removing BrushTool's cached duplicate ParticleBrush(self.app.sim) does not alter physics behavior; (4) the deliberate non-policed exception preserving Scene.update's direct sketch.interaction_data read at scene.py:324 is the correct boundary — Scene is model orchestrator, not a UI tool. No changes to simulation arrays, Numba kernels (solver_kernels.py), integration, spatial hashing, atomization, or tether force math. Physics correctness and performance characteristics are preserved. The bundled Air Gap completion is sound from a Simulation-domain perspective; doing it before further Flow State CRs is the right ordering.

Independent technical review of CR-112 v0.3 in tu_ui domain (UI/tools).

VERIFICATION OF v0.1 FINDINGS:

Finding #1 (blocking): subclass property accessors at tools.py:277-283 and 741-747 — addressed in v0.2, preserved in v0.3. §3.1 'Hidden coupling' table and §5.2 prescribe rewriting both property bodies to delegate via ctx._get_sketch()/_get_scene(). Verified properties exist at the cited lines.

Finding #2 (blocking): ctx.clear_constraint_ui defects (uses btn.is_active vs actual btn.active; partial manual state clear vs builder.reset()). Independently verified at tool_context.py:474-486 — both defects present in current code. §5.4 fix is correct: replaces body with builder.reset() + iteration over constraint_btn_map setting btn.active=False. Behavior matches the working tools.py:1378-1384 pattern.

Finding #3 (blocking): set_interaction_data signature mismatch — tools.py:978 sets point_idx but the ctx method only accepts handle_t. §5.3.1 fix extends to keyword-only (handle_t=None, point_idx=None). The two set callsites in §5.3 use keyword form. Backward-compatible per CR's claim (no existing callers; method currently unused).

Finding #5 (non-blocking, surfaced by QA in v0.2 cycle): pre-deletion audit for cached self.app sub-objects. Now addressed in §5.2 final paragraph. I independently re-ran the equivalent grep — exact matches: tools.py:87 (self.app = ctx._app) and source_tool.py:33 (self.app = app). No other cached sub-objects (self.session, self.layout, etc.) exist. Audit is accurate; migration scope is complete.

VERIFICATION OF v0.2-CYCLE QA FINDINGS (A/B/C):

(A) §2.3 ↔ §7.1 sync. Cross-checked counts: source_tool.py = 25 self.app. reaches (verified by grep), tools.py = 99 (verified). Both sections list the same files and same change set including the six items in the v0.3 revision summary (property-accessor migration, BrushTool ParticleBrush replacement, _clear_constraint_ui body replacement, ctx.clear_constraint_ui fix, set_interaction_data signature extension, has_interaction_data addition). Sync confirmed.

(B) Wrong dependency claim dropped. Independently verified source_tool.py contains no calls to clear_constraint_ui or builder.reset(). EI-5 in §10 cleanly references only ctx.add_process_object() as its EI-4 dependency. Correct.

(C) Cached-sub-object audit added at §5.2. See Finding #5 above — verified accurate.

ADDITIONAL CHECKS IN MY DOMAIN:

- Air Gap principle correctly upheld: deliberate non-policed exception at §5.3 for Scene.update reading interaction_data is principled (Scene is not a tool).
- Input chain order at input_handler.py:113-129 is System → Modals → Global → HUD; matches §5.5 docstring fix and §5.6.2 CLAUDE.md correction.
- BrushTool ParticleBrush migration: Scene owns its own ParticleBrush (scene.py:82) and delegates paint/erase. Removing BrushTool's local cached brush eliminates duplicate state without changing end behavior. Architectural improvement is real.
- §5.5 docstring drift fixes (input_handler 5-layer wording; compiler ONE-WAY wording) verified against current source.
- EI ordering (EI-4 ToolContext API → EI-5 SourceTool → EI-6 tools.py → EI-7 passthrough deletion → EI-8 docstrings) is sound; deleting the passthrough before completing per-tool migration would crash unmigrated tools, and the CR explicitly calls this out.

VERDICT: Recommend.

The draft is technically accurate, internally consistent, and complete within my domain. All three of my original blocking v0.1 findings remain addressed; my non-blocking v0.1 finding #5 is now properly addressed. The v0.2-cycle inconsistencies (A/B/C) are resolved cleanly. Ready to advance.
